SYM_NMC_CULTIVATION_OF_MYCOBACTERIUM_TUB.name: Cultivation Of Mycobacterium Tuberculosis In Cerebrospinal Fluid

SYM_NMC_PATHOGENIC_BACTERIA_FOUND_IN_CER.name: Pathogenic Bacteria Found In Cerebrospinal Fluid

SYM_NMC_INCREASED_PROTEIN_LEVELS_IN_CERE.name: Increased Protein Levels In Cerebrospinal Fluid

SYM_NMC_ELEVATED_WHITE_BLOOD_CELL_COUNT_.name: Elevated White Blood Cell Count In Cerebrospinal Fluid

SYM_NMC_DECREASED_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUID_SU.name: Decreased Cerebrospinal Fluid Sugar Content And Chloride Levels

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTIC_SYMPTOMS.name: Psychotic Symptoms
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTIC_SYMPTOMS.description: Patients exhibit significant and persistent abnormalities in cognition, emotional regulation, or behavior, with a disruption in the integrity and coherence of mental activities. Their reality testing and social functioning are severely impaired, leading to an inability to adapt to normal life, accompanied by a lack of insight.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_PLMI.name: PSG: Repeated Periodic Limb Movements
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_PLMI.description: The patient exhibits ≥5 periodic limb movements per hour during sleep, which supports the diagnosis of restless legs syndrome (RLS).

SYM_NEW_PSY_RESTLESS_LEGS.name: Restless Legs Syndrome
SYM_NEW_PSY_RESTLESS_LEGS.description: Restless legs syndrome (RLS) is characterized by an intense discomfort in the lower limbs, primarily occurring during nighttime sleep or when at rest. Patients frequently describe this abnormal sensation as crawling, tingling, itching, or aching.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOESTHESIA.name: Hypoesthesia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOESTHESIA.description: Hypoesthesia is defined as a reduction in sensory perception, characterized by an increased threshold for detecting external stimuli. Patients with hypoesthesia may experience a diminished sensitivity to strong stimuli or may be unable to perceive sensations altogether (sensory loss).

SYM_NEW_PSY_POSSIBLE_MENTAL_DISORDERS.name: Suspicious Mental Disorders
SYM_NEW_PSY_POSSIBLE_MENTAL_DISORDERS.description: The patient exhibits signs suggestive of a psychiatric disorder or mental health condition. It is advisable to refer them for evaluation by a psychiatrist or request a psychiatric consultation.。

SYM_NEW_PSY_INVOLUNTARY_MOVEMENT.name: Involuntary Movements
SYM_NEW_PSY_INVOLUNTARY_MOVEMENT.description: Involuntary movements refer to abnormal motor activities that occur in patients who are fully conscious but cannot consciously control or direct these movements.

SYM_NEW_PSY_LOW_LEVELS_OF_OREXIN_IN_CERE.name: Low Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) Orexin Levels
SYM_NEW_PSY_LOW_LEVELS_OF_OREXIN_IN_CERE.description: The patient has low levels of orexin in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF).

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_BARCOLEPSY.name: PSG: Narcolepsy
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_BARCOLEPSY.description: Presence of REM sleep within a short period after sleep onset (typically within 15 minutes), which is unusual in healthy individuals.

SYM_NEW_PSY_MSLT_BARCOLEPSY.name: MSLT: Narcolepsy
SYM_NEW_PSY_MSLT_BARCOLEPSY.description: The Multiple Sleep Latency Test (MSLT) shows a shortened average sleep latency and the presence of two or more sleep-onset REM periods (SOREMPs).

SYM_NEW_PSY_CATAPLEXY.name: Cataplexy
SYM_NEW_PSY_CATAPLEXY.description: Cataplexy refers to a sudden and brief episode of muscle weakness or paralysis caused by a complete or partial loss of bilateral muscle tone, resulting in generalized or localized flaccid attacks. These episodes can manifest as falls, head droop, eyelid droop, mouth opening, or tongue protrusion.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_PARALYSIS.name: Sleep Paralysis
SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_PARALYSIS.description: Sleep paralysis is a transient condition that occurs during the transition between sleep and wakefulness. It is characterized by a temporary inability to move or speak, despite being fully conscious. Episodes typically last for a few seconds to a few minutes.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_RELATED_HALLUCINARION.name: Sleep Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_RELATED_HALLUCINARION.description: Sleep hallucinations encompass a range of phenomena, including hypnagogic (occurring while falling asleep) and hypnopompic (occurring upon waking) hallucinations. These experiences involve vivid sensory perceptions, which can be visual, auditory, or tactile.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DIVERGENCE_OF_ATTENTION.name: Attention Dispersion
SYM_NEW_PSY_DIVERGENCE_OF_ATTENTION.description: Divergence of Attention refers to a marked reduction in the ability to focus and sustain attention on a specific object or task.

SYM_NEW_PSY_CEREBROVASCULAR_DISEASE.name: Cerebrovascular Disease
SYM_NEW_PSY_CEREBROVASCULAR_DISEASE.description: The patient has cerebrovascular disease, including but not limited to cerebral infarction, cerebral hemorrhage, leukoaraiosis, and chronic cerebral ischemia.

SYM_NEW_PSY_CEREBRAL_ATROPHY.name: Cerebral Atrophy
SYM_NEW_PSY_CEREBRAL_ATROPHY.description: Cerebral Atrophy refers to the imaging manifestation of abnormal reduction in brain cells and the shrinkage of brain tissue. It can be a sign of normal aging or result from various causes such as brain injury, neurological diseases, or infections.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ORGANIC_PERSONALITY_CHANGE.name: Organic Personality Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_ORGANIC_PERSONALITY_CHANGE.description: Organic Personality Change specifically results from organic brain pathology and is considered part of the primary disease symptoms.

SYM_NEW_PSY_APRAXIA.name: Apraxia
SYM_NEW_PSY_APRAXIA.description: Apraxia is a condition in which an individual, despite having normal sensory function, muscle strength, and coordination, is unable to perform purposeful movements.

SYM_NEW_PSY_AGNOSIA.name: Agnosia
SYM_NEW_PSY_AGNOSIA.description: Agnosia is a neurological disorder characterized by the inability to recognize or identify objects, sounds, or other sensory stimuli despite having intact sensory function. This impairment occurs at the cortical level of the brain, and is not due to damage to peripheral sensory organs such as hearing loss.

SYM_NEW_PSY_APHASIA.name: Aphasia
SYM_NEW_PSY_APHASIA.description: Aphasia refers to the loss of the ability to understand or express language due to localized damage in certain areas of the brain. Patients with aphasia have clear consciousness and do not suffer from sensory deficits or paralysis of the speech muscles.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_INSOMNIA.name: PSG：Insomnia
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_INSOMNIA.description: Polysomnography (PSG) revealed that the patient had prolonged sleep latency, extended wake after sleep onset, reduced sleep efficiency, and shortened objective sleep time. These findings are consistent with a diagnosis of insomnia. No evidence of sleep-disordered breathing was observed.

SYM_NEW_PSY_VERBAL_AUDITORY_HALLUCINATIO.name: Auditory Verbal Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_VERBAL_AUDITORY_HALLUCINATIO.description: Auditory verbal hallucinations specifically involve hearing voices or sounds that resemble human speech.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMENT_HALLUCINATION.name: Commentary Auditory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMENT_HALLUCINATION.description: Commentary auditory hallucinations specifically refer to auditory hallucinations where the content involves judgments or criticisms about the individual's moral character, behavior, or actions. These hallucinations often include reprimands, ridicule, or sarcasm.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ARGUMENT_HALLUCINATION.name: Argumentative Auditory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_ARGUMENT_HALLUCINATION.description: Argumentative auditory hallucinations refer to a type of auditory hallucination where the individual hears voices engaged in arguments or debates. These voices may be perceived as arguing with each other or with the person experiencing the hallucinations.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMAND_HALLUCINATION.name: Command Auditory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMAND_HALLUCINATION.description: Command auditory hallucinations can indeed lead to severe outcomes, including self-harm and violence toward others.

SYM_NEW_PSY_VISUAL_HALLUCINATION.name: Visual Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_VISUAL_HALLUCINATION.description: Visual hallucinations refer to the experience of seeing visual images or scenes that are not actually present in the external environment. These hallucinations can vary greatly in their content and detail.

SYM_NEW_PSY_OLFACTORY_HALLUCINATION.name: Olfactory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_OLFACTORY_HALLUCINATION.description: Olfactory hallucinations refer to the experience of smelling odors that are not present in the environment. These hallucinations can involve a variety of smells, often leading to distressing emotional responses.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_PERSEECUTION.name: Paranoid Delusion
SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_PERSEECUTION.description: Paranoid Delusion is a type of thought disorder characterized by a fixed, unfounded belief that others are persecuting or conspiring against the individual or their loved ones.

SYM_NEW_PSY_APATHY.name: Apathy
SYM_NEW_PSY_APATHY.description: Apathy refers to a reduction in emotional responsiveness to external stimuli, characterized by a diminished emotional reaction. This condition manifests as a notable decrease in emotional engagement and expression.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_ACT.name: Compulsive Behavior
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_ACT.description: Compulsive behavior refers to actions or mental activities that a patient feels compelled to repeat, often to alleviate or counteract the discomfort and anxiety caused by obsessive thoughts. These repetitive behaviors are driven by the need to prevent or neutralize perceived threats or fears associated with obsessive ideas.

SYM_NEW_PSY_EUPHORIA.name: Euphoria
SYM_NEW_PSY_EUPHORIA.description: Euphoria is characterized by a state of excessive self-satisfaction and pleasure that is disconnected from the surrounding environment. Individuals may feel inexplicably happy, often without a clear reason for their elevated mood. The content of this euphoria is typically repetitive and lacks depth. The facial expressions of those experiencing euphoria are often joyful but can appear foolish or silly, failing to resonate with others.

SYM_NEW_PSY_EMOTIONAL_FRAGILITY.name: Emotional Vulnerability
SYM_NEW_PSY_EMOTIONAL_FRAGILITY.description: Emotional vulnerability refers to an exaggerated emotional response to minor external stimuli. Individuals with this condition experience heightened sensitivity to emotional triggers, resulting in intense and sometimes uncontrollable emotional reactions.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DELIRIUM.name: Delirium
SYM_NEW_PSY_DELIRIUM.description: Delirium, also known as acute brain syndrome or acute organic syndrome, is characterized by a fundamental disturbance in consciousness and overall mental activity. It manifests with a decreased clarity of consciousness and is accompanied by various cognitive impairments.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSIVE_SYNDROME.name: Depressive Syndrome
SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSIVE_SYNDROME.description: Patients with depressive syndrome experience a persistent state of low mood, characterized by a significant decrease in interest and energy, and a notable lack of pleasure.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ANXIETY_TENSION_SYNDROME.name: Anxiety Syndrome
SYM_NEW_PSY_ANXIETY_TENSION_SYNDROME.description: Anxiety syndrome, also known as generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), is characterized by a predominant cluster of anxiety symptoms.

SYM_NEW_PSY_INSOMNIA.name: Athens Insomnia Scale
SYM_NEW_PSY_INSOMNIA.description: The patient had clinically significant insomnia as measured by the Athens Insomnia Scale.。

SYM_NEW_PSY_IMPAIRED_COGNITIVE_FUNCTION.name: Cognitive Impairment
SYM_NEW_PSY_IMPAIRED_COGNITIVE_FUNCTION.description: Cognitive impairment refers to a decline in one or more cognitive domains, such as memory, attention, executive function, or problem-solving skills.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPONESIA.name: Hyponesia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPONESIA.description: Hyponesia refers to a generalized decline in the functioning of all basic memory processes, characterized by a reduction in recent memory. In more severe cases, remote memory is also affected.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DIFFICULTY_FALLING_ASLEEP.name: Difficulty Falling Asleep
SYM_NEW_PSY_DIFFICULTY_FALLING_ASLEEP.description: Difficulty Falling Asleep refers to the difficulty or prolonged effort required to initiate sleep. Individuals experiencing this symptom may struggle to fall asleep despite feeling tired or having an adequate sleep opportunity.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_MAINTENANCE_DISORDER.name: Sleep Maintenance Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_MAINTENANCE_DISORDER.description: Patients have difficulty maintaining sleep and often have difficulty falling asleep again after awakening in the middle of the night.

SYM_NEW_PSY_EARLY_AWAKENING.name: Early Awakening
SYM_NEW_PSY_EARLY_AWAKENING.description: Early Awakening refers to the condition where a person wakes up earlier than desired or expected, often significantly earlier than their intended wake-up time, and is unable to return to sleep.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DAYTIME_SLEEPINESS.name: Daytime Sleepiness
SYM_NEW_PSY_DAYTIME_SLEEPINESS.description: Daytime Sleepiness refers to a condition where patients struggle to maintain alertness and wakefulness during the daytime, despite needing to stay awake and attentive.

EXM_NEW_PSY_OREXIN_IN_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUI.name: Orexin In Cerebrospinal Fluid Test
EXM_NEW_PSY_OREXIN_IN_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUI.description: By sampling cerebrospinal fluid, this test determines whether the concentration of orexin-1 in the cerebrospinal fluid is ≤100 pg/ml or lower than 1/3 of the normal value, used to diagnose typical episodes of narcolepsy with cataplexy. It is particularly beneficial for patients who are difficult to undergo MSLT due to cost or inability to cooperate with the examination, and who are using psychotropic drugs and are difficult to discontinue.

EXM_NEW_PSY_MSLT.name: Multiple Sleep Latency Test (MSLT)
EXM_NEW_PSY_MSLT.description: Requires advanced diagnosis. Multiple Sleep Latency Test (MSLT) is the most commonly used standard protocol for objectively evaluating daytime sleepiness in clinical practice and research, often used for narcolepsy or sleep-related breathing disorders.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSG.name: Polysomnography（PSG）
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSG.description: Requires advanced diagnosis. Polysomnography (PSG) system monitors breathing, arterial oxygen saturation, electroencephalogram (EEG), electrocardiogram (ECG), heart rate, and other indicators continuously during the night to detect sleep abnormalities and diagnose sleep disorders, especially sleep-related breathing disorders. Although PSG can reflect the condition of patients with chronic insomnia, the diagnosis of chronic insomnia mainly relies on the subjective report of patients. PSG is only used as a means to exclude other sleep disorders, rather than a routine examination for insomnia.

EXM_NEW_PSY_INTERVIEW_SLEEP.name: Psychiatric Exam - Sleep
EXM_NEW_PSY_INTERVIEW_SLEEP.description: Understanding the patient's sleep through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_BDI.name: Beck's Depression Inventory (BDI)
EXM_NEW_PSY_BDI.description: Beck Depression Inventory (BDI) is a self-rating scale developed by the renowned American psychologist Beck in the 1960s. It is available in two versions: BDI-13 and BDI-21. Considering that severely depressed patients may have difficulty completing 21 items, Beck introduced BDI-13 in 1973, which is now widely used.

EXM_NEW_PSY_SAS.name: Anxiety Self-Rating Scale (SAS)
EXM_NEW_PSY_SAS.description: The Anxiety Self-Rating Scale was developed by Zung in 1971. Similar to the Self-Rating Depression Scale (SDS), it consists of 20 items used to assess the subjective feelings of anxious patients, with good validity.

EXM_NEW_PSY_AIS.name: Athens Insomnia Scale (AIS)
EXM_NEW_PSY_AIS.description: The Athens Insomnia Scale (AIS), is an internationally recognized self-assessment tool for measuring sleep quality, widely used.

EXM_NEW_PSY_MMSE.name: Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE)
EXM_NEW_PSY_MMSE.description: Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) was developed by Folstein et al. in 1975. It is currently the most widely used and commonly employed cognitive screening tool in the world. It is simple to administer and has good reliability and validity.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_CONS.name: Psychiatric Exam - Consciousness
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_CONS.description: Understanding the patient's level of consciousness through psychiatric exam

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERS.name: Psychiatric Exam - Personality
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERS.description: Understanding the patient's personality through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MEMO.name: Psychiatric Exam - Memory
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MEMO.description: Understanding the patient's memory through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERC.name: Psychiatric Exam - Perception
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERC.description: Understanding the patient's perception through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_EMOT.name: Psychiatric Exam - Emotion
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_EMOT.description: Understanding the patient's emotion through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MOVE.name: Psychiatric Exam - Movement
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MOVE.description: Understanding the patient's movement through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_THOU.name: Psychiatric Exam - Thoughts
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_THOU.description: Understanding the patient's thoughts through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_ATTE.name: Psychiatric Exam - Attention
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_ATTE.description: Understanding the patient's attention through psychiatric exam.

TRT_NMC_CARBAMAZEPINE.name: Carbamazepine
TRT_NMC_CARBAMAZEPINE.description: Carbamazepine is an anticonvulsant and mood stabilizer. In neurology, it is the first-line treatment for partial seizures, with superior efficacy for complex partial seizures compared to other antiepileptic drugs. It is also effective in treating secondary generalized tonic-clonic seizures but may exacerbate absence and myoclonic seizures. In psychiatry, carbamazepine is used for the treatment of acute manic episodes in bipolar disorder, particularly in patients who are unresponsive to lithium, or those experiencing rapid cycling or mixed episodes. Additionally, carbamazepine is employed in the management of certain types of neuropathic pain. It is important to note that carbamazepine is an inducer of various drug-metabolizing enzymes, which can lead to increased metabolism of concomitant medications, thereby reducing their plasma concentrations and potentially diminishing their therapeutic effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_CALCIUM_CHANNEL_REGULATOR_PR.name: Pregabalin
TRT_NEW_PSY_CALCIUM_CHANNEL_REGULATOR_PR.description: Pregabalin is a calcium channel modulator and an anticonvulsant medication. It is commonly used to treat various types of neuropathic pain and chronic pain conditions. Additionally, it is considered a first-line treatment for generalized anxiety disorder (GAD).

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTICHOLINERGICS_TRIHEXYPHEN.name: Benztropine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTICHOLINERGICS_TRIHEXYPHEN.description: Benztropine is an anticholinergic medication that selectively blocks cholinergic neural pathways in the striatum while exerting minimal peripheral effects. This action helps restore the balance between dopamine and acetylcholine in the brain. It is used in the treatment of Parkinson's disease, Parkinsonism, and drug-induced extrapyramidal disorders. In other words, benztropine can counteract the extrapyramidal side effects caused by typical antipsychotic medications.

TRT_NEW_PSY_LEVODOPA_AND_BENSERAZIDE_HYD.name: Cabergoline
TRT_NEW_PSY_LEVODOPA_AND_BENSERAZIDE_HYD.description: Cabergoline is a combination formulation of levodopa and benserazide. Levodopa, a precursor in dopamine synthesis, can cross the blood-brain barrier and is used to increase dopamine levels in the brain, making it effective for treating Parkinson's disease, which is characterized by a deficiency of dopamine in the basal ganglia. Additionally, cabergoline can be used for treating restless legs syndrome, potentially due to its effect on normalizing dopaminergic abnormalities in the central nervous system. However, long-term use may exacerbate symptoms, so it should be administered only as needed. The levodopa component of the combination can cause gastrointestinal adverse effects, cardiovascular adverse effects, dyskinesias (involuntary movements), and psychiatric disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PRAMIPEXOLE.name: Pramipexole
TRT_NEW_PSY_PRAMIPEXOLE.description: Pramipexole is a non-ergot, selective dopamine receptor agonist that specifically stimulates dopamine D2 receptors, with minimal action on D1 receptors and low affinity for adrenergic α2 receptors. It is commonly used in the treatment of Parkinson's disease. Due to its long half-life, pramipexole does not require nighttime dosing and is well-tolerated with minimal complications during long-term use. It effectively alleviates over 90% of symptoms in restless legs syndrome (RLS), making it a first-line treatment option for RLS, with pramipexole being the only approved medication for this condition. Initial treatment may be associated with orthostatic hypotension and gastrointestinal adverse effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_VITAMIN_C.name: Vitamin C
TRT_NEW_PSY_VITAMIN_C.description: Vitamin C plays a crucial role in antibody and collagen formation, tissue repair (including certain redox reactions), metabolism of phenylalanine, tyrosine, and folate, utilization of iron and carbohydrates, synthesis of fats and proteins, and maintenance of immune function. It is also essential for the hydroxylation of serotonin, maintaining vascular integrity, and enhancing the absorption of non-heme iron. Vitamin C is commonly used for dietary supplementation, chronic dialysis, prevention and treatment of vitamin C deficiency, acidifying urine, and idiopathic erythrocytosis. Additionally, vitamin C can enhance iron absorption when used in conjunction with iron supplements.

TRT_NEW_PSY_TCAs_CLOMIPRAMINE.name: Clomipramine
TRT_NEW_PSY_TCAs_CLOMIPRAMINE.description: Clomipramine, also known as Anafranil, is a traditional tricyclic antidepressant. Due to its broad spectrum of side effects, it is now infrequently used for depression and is primarily considered a second-line treatment option for obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD).

TRT_NEW_PSY_OTHER_NEW_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DR.name: Mirtazapine
TRT_NEW_PSY_OTHER_NEW_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DR.description: Mirtazapine is the only new antidepressant with strong antihistaminic effects, which helps in sedation and appetite stimulation. Similar to tricyclic antidepressants and SSRIs, mirtazapine is also a potent REM sleep suppressant, which can reduce nighttime awakenings and extend total sleep duration. The most common side effects of mirtazapine include excessive sedation, increased appetite, weight gain, and dry mouth.

TRT_NMC_AMITRIPTYLINE.name: Amitriptyline
TRT_NMC_AMITRIPTYLINE.description: Amitriptyline is a class of tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) that can inhibit the reuptake of monoamine neurotransmitters and have strong sedative and anticholinergic effects. In the field of psychiatry, the tricyclic antidepressants to which amitriptyline belongs are now rarely used, but it has been found in clinical practice that amitriptyline is more effective in treating physical symptoms; In other specialized fields, amitriptyline is often used for the prevention and treatment of migraine and neuropathic pain, as well as for functional disorders such as irritable bowel syndrome and cardiac neurosis. In addition, the SNRI antidepressant drug duloxetine has been found to have good therapeutic effects on various physical discomforts and chronic pain.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_COMPOU.name: Fluphenazine-Melitracen Tablets
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_COMPOU.description: Fluphenazine-Melitracen Tablets is a combination medication that includes a typical antipsychotic (fluphenazine) and a tricyclic antidepressant (melitracen). It is approved for the treatment of mild to moderate depression and anxiety symptoms, including traditional neurotic disorders or neuroticism, and is used for various functional somatic symptoms or psychosomatic disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome, functional dyspepsia, chronic gastritis, and cardiac neurosis. The medication is known for its relatively rapid onset of action. However, it is considered to have limited long-term efficacy and may cause serious adverse effects, such as tardive dyskinesia, and significant withdrawal reactions. Therefore, it is not recommended as a routine medication for depression, anxiety disorders, or similar conditions in major clinical guidelines.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SARIS_.name: Trazodone
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SARIS_.description: Trazodone is the first of the second-generation antidepressants. Its primary pharmacological effect is mediated through postsynaptic 5-HT2 receptors. Although it has weak effects on the 5-HT transporter, Ach receptors, and H1 receptors, these can be considered negligible. It does not have significant activity on NE and DA receptors but is an antagonist at peripheral α-adrenergic receptors, which can lead to side effects such as orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, dry mouth, and priapism. Trazodone is a choice for the acute treatment of depression, demonstrating efficacy comparable to that of tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) and other new antidepressants. It also helps improve sleep, with its sedative effects potentially providing rapid relief for patients with severe insomnia or anxiety. The most common side effect of trazodone is drowsiness.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_AGOMEL.name: Agomelatine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_AGOMEL.description: In psychiatry, agomelatine is the first antidepressant that acts on melatonin receptors. Agomelatine likely exerts its antidepressant effects by activating the MT1 and MT2 receptors, which helps synchronize circadian rhythms. Additionally, agomelatine is an antagonist at the 5-HT2C receptor, which blocks the receptor's inhibitory effect on norepinephrine (NE) and dopamine (DA) release, thereby increasing their concentrations in the prefrontal cortex and contributing to its antidepressant properties. In the field of sleep medicine, agomelatine has the ability to regulate circadian rhythms and sleep cycles. It effectively improves sleep continuity and increases the proportion of slow-wave sleep, leading to a normalization of overnight slow-wave and delta wave distribution, thereby enhancing overall sleep quality without altering REM sleep. Overall, patients generally tolerate agomelatine well, with no significant withdrawal symptoms observed.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_NDRIS_.name: Bupropion
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_NDRIS_.description: Bupropion is a novel antidepressant whose pharmacological mechanism remains somewhat controversial. It can be classified as a norepinephrine and dopamine reuptake inhibitor (NDRI). Bupropion has antidepressant effects but lacks anxiolytic properties, making it commonly used for treating depression and smoking cessation.  However, it may lead to central nervous system activation symptoms, with insomnia being one of the most common adverse effects. Anxiety and depression may also occur.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIEPILEPTICS_TOPIRAMATE.name: Topiramate
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIEPILEPTICS_TOPIRAMATE.description: Topiramate, marketed under the brand name Topamax, is an anticonvulsant medication used primarily for the treatment of epilepsy. In pediatric psychiatry, it is utilized as a treatment option for refractory tic disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_AWAKENING_PROMOTING_DRUGS_PI.name: Tilopidine
TRT_NEW_PSY_AWAKENING_PROMOTING_DRUGS_PI.description: Tilopidine is a recently approved selective histamine H3 receptor inverse agonist. By inhibiting the negative feedback mechanism, it increases histamine release and activates histaminergic neurons, resulting in an arousal effect. As a novel and potent wakefulness-promoting agent, tilopidine has not shown significant addiction potential to date.

TRT_NEW_PSY_NRIS_TOMOXETINE.name: Atomoxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_NRIS_TOMOXETINE.description: Atomoxetine, marketed under the brand name Strattera, is a non-stimulant medication used to treat attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD). Specifically, it is a selective norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (NRI). Atomoxetine is non-addictive and provides effects that are either slightly less potent than or comparable to those of stimulants like methylphenidate. It has demonstrated efficacy and good tolerance over a one-year follow-up period. Initially found to be effective in adults, Atomoxetine was later approved for use in children aged 6 years and older.

TRT_NEW_PSY_TCM_TIANZHI_GRANULES.name: Tianzhi Granules
TRT_NEW_PSY_TCM_TIANZHI_GRANULES.description: Tianzhi Granules consist of a combination of Gastrodia Elata, Uncaria Rhynchophylla, Fossilia Ossis Chinensis, Eucommia Ulmoides, Taxillus Chinensis, Poria Cocos, Fallopia Multiflora, Sophora Japonica, Gardenia Jasminoides, Scutellaria Baicalensis, Achyranthes Bidentata, and Leonurus Japonicus. This formulation is designed to calm the liver and subdue yang, nourish the liver and kidneys, and enhance cognitive function. It is used for treating cognitive decline, poor memory, slowed thinking, and disorientation resulting from liver yang rising, along with symptoms such as dizziness, headaches, irritability, insomnia, bitter mouth, dry throat, and lower back and knee weakness. This remedy is indicated for mild to moderate vascular dementia associated with liver yang rising syndrome.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BRAIN_FUNCTION_IMPROVEMENT_D.name: Nimodipine
TRT_NEW_PSY_BRAIN_FUNCTION_IMPROVEMENT_D.description: Nimodipine is a cerebrovascular agent and one of the most widely used neuroprotective drugs in clinical practice. It readily crosses the blood-brain barrier and acts on cerebral blood vessels and neuronal cells, selectively dilating cerebral vessels without causing "steal" phenomena. Nimodipine also antagonizes various factors contributing to cerebral vasospasm, including K+, 5-HT, arachidonic acid, hydrogen peroxide, TXA2, PGF2α, and vasospasm following subarachnoid hemorrhage.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BRAIN_FUNCTION_IMPROVEMENT_D.name: Oxiracetam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BRAIN_FUNCTION_IMPROVEMENT_D.description: Oxiracetam is a nootropic drug classified as a new-generation cognitive enhancer. It works by promoting the synthesis of phosphatidylcholine and phosphatidylethanolamine, thereby increasing the ATP/ADP ratio in the brain. This action enhances protein and nucleic acid synthesis, which in turn improves brain metabolism. Oxiracetam is used to counteract brain function damage and memory impairment caused by physical and chemical factors. It has been shown to improve retrograde amnesia due to hypoxia and may enhance memory and learning functions in patients with various types of senile dementia and memory disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BETA_BLOCKERS_PROPRANOLOL.name: Propranolol
TRT_NEW_PSY_BETA_BLOCKERS_PROPRANOLOL.description: Propranolol is a beta-blocker used to treat various arrhythmias, hypertension, and tremors. In the field of psychiatry, while propranolol has minimal direct effects on anxiety itself, it can alleviate the physical and physiological symptoms of anxiety, such as palpitations and tremors. As a result, it is sometimes used to manage performance anxiety in social phobia or social anxiety disorder, though its efficacy for generalized anxiety disorder remains a subject of debate.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_SODIUM_VALPR.name: Sodium Valproate
TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_SODIUM_VALPR.description: Sodium valproate (brand name: Divalproex) is an anticonvulsant and mood stabilizer used in the treatment of manic episodes in bipolar disorder, particularly effective for rapid cycling and mixed episodes. Its efficacy in preventing relapse of bipolar disorder is comparable to that of lithium, making it a suitable alternative to lithium. Additionally, sodium valproate is commonly used for managing and treating behavioral symptoms associated with dementia, such as euphoria that impacts daily functioning.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_lAMOTRIGINE.name: Lamotrigine
TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_lAMOTRIGINE.description: Lamotrigine is traditionally known as an antiepileptic drug, but it has also emerged as a mood stabilizer, particularly effective for the depressive phase of bipolar disorder. Additionally, there is anecdotal evidence suggesting that lamotrigine may be effective in treating depersonalization, potentially alleviating symptoms such as derealization, somatic depersonalization, and attentional disturbances associated with the condition.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM_IV.name: Intravenous Infusion Of Diazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM_IV.description: Diazepam is a benzodiazepine medication. Intravenous injection can rapidly alleviate panic attacks and relieve anxiety states. However, it is crucial to administer it slowly to avoid the risk of respiratory depression.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_METHYLPHENI.name: Methylphenidate
TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_METHYLPHENI.description: Methylphenidate, also known as piperidine methyl ester and commercially available as Concerta (extended-release) and Ritalin (immediate-release), is a central nervous system stimulant. It acts directly on the brain to enhance cortical activity, improve mental alertness, alleviate fatigue, and boost overall mental energy. It is used to manage symptoms of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and excessive daytime sleepiness related to narcolepsy.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_MODAFINIL.name: Modafinil
TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_MODAFINIL.description: Modafinil is a novel non-amphetamine central nervous system stimulant primarily used for the treatment of various types of excessive sleepiness disorders, such as narcolepsy, obstructive sleep apnea, and shift work sleep disorder. Compared to traditional stimulants, it generally has milder side effects. However, long-term use still carries a potential risk of drug dependence.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_DIAZEPAM.name: Diazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_DIAZEPAM.description: Diazepam, also known as Valium, is a long-acting, medium-potency benzodiazepine with the typical benzodiazepine properties of anxiolytic, sedative-hypnotic, and antiepileptic effects. Diazepam is valued for its rapid onset, safety, and effectiveness. However, like other benzodiazepines, it poses risks of dependence, addiction, and misuse with prolonged or improper use. Additionally, diazepam is frequently used in the management of alcohol withdrawal.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_LORAZEPAM.name: Lorazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_LORAZEPAM.description: Lorazepam, also known as Lora, is a medium-to-short-acting benzodiazepine with potent anxiolytic and hypnotic effects. It is recognized for having one of the strongest anxiolytic effects among benzodiazepines, and it also provides sedative benefits. Lorazepam offers several advantages, including rapid onset of action, safety, and efficacy. However, prolonged or improper use can lead to dependence, addiction, and misuse. In addition to its use in treating anxiety disorders, lorazepam is also commonly used for managing agitation and tension in conditions such as schizophrenia.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Sertraline
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Sertraline is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) with antidepressant, anxiolytic, and anti-obsessive properties. Due to its relatively low incidence of adverse effects, it is frequently used in children, adolescents, and older adults, particularly for symptoms associated with organic mental disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Fluvoxamine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Fluvoxamine is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) that is approved for the treatment of depression and obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD). Clinically, among SSRIs, fluvoxamine is noted for its particularly effective treatment of OCD.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Paroxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Paroxetine, marketed under the brand name Paxil, is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI). Clinically, it is used to treat depression, particularly major depressive disorder with anxiety, as well as generalized anxiety disorder, panic disorder, and obsessive-compulsive disorder.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MECT.name: MECT
TRT_NEW_PSY_MECT.description: Modified Electroconvulsive Therapy (MECT) is a treatment method that involves inducing a generalized seizure with a small amount of electrical current under general anesthesia and with the support of oxygenation. It is primarily used for the treatment of major depressive episodes, but it can also be employed for mood disorders, schizophrenia, and for the treatment of neuroleptic malignant syndrome or serotonin syndrome induced by antipsychotic or antidepressant medications.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_TIAPRIDE.name: Sulpride
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_TIAPRIDE.description: Sulpride, marketed as Thiapride, is a traditional benzamide antipsychotic that is not approved in Canada, the United States, or the United Kingdom but is more commonly used in Europe and China. It primarily acts as a dopamine antagonist, mainly targeting the midbrain and limbic system. Sulpride is used for conditions such as Tourette syndrome, chorea, and geriatric psychosis. Common side effects include drowsiness, gastrointestinal reactions, and dizziness or fatigue. It has a lower incidence of extrapyramidal side effects and may cause moderate, transient hyperprolactinemia. Notably, it does not affect cognitive functions or neurophysiological parameters in children, nor does it interfere with the hypothalamic-pituitary regulation of various hormones.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_RISPERIDO.name: Risperidone
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_RISPERIDO.description: Risperidone, a second-generation antipsychotic introduced after clozapine, is now available in a long-acting injectable formulation. It exhibits strong antagonistic effects on central 5-HT receptors, particularly 5-HT2A, and on D2 receptors. Its antagonistic effect on D2 receptors is similar to that of haloperidol. Additionally, risperidone has high affinity for α1 and α2 receptors but lower affinity for β receptors and muscarinic cholinergic receptors. Consequently, it shares similar efficacy with typical antipsychotics in treating positive symptoms of schizophrenia while also showing good effectiveness for negative symptoms. Risperidone has minimal sedative effects and lacks significant anticholinergic side effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_ARIPIPRAZ.name: Aripiprazole
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_ARIPIPRAZ.description: Aripiprazole is an atypical antipsychotic and is considered the only third-generation antipsychotic currently available. It functions as a unique serotonin-dopamine activity modulator. Aripiprazole exhibits partial agonistic effects on post-synaptic dopamine D2 receptors, and when dopamine (DA) activity is elevated, it helps reduce DA activity to alleviate positive symptoms of schizophrenia. Aripiprazole is indicated for the treatment of schizophrenia and has recently gained widespread recognition for its efficacy in treating Tourette's disorder, particularly Tourette's syndrome. The drug is noted for its effectiveness and relatively mild side effect profile, including minimal sedation and a low incidence of extrapyramidal symptoms or hyperprolactinemia.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_OLANZAPIN.name: Olanzapine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_OLANZAPIN.description: Olanzapine is a derivative of clozapine and is classified as a second-generation antipsychotic. It is a multi-receptor antagonist that blocks 5-HT2A, D2, D1, and D4 receptors, as well as muscarinic cholinergic receptors (M1), H1 histamine receptors, 5-HT3 receptors, and α1 adrenergic receptors. Its blockade of 5-HT2A receptors is approximately eight times more potent than its blockade of dopamine receptors. Although its pharmacological profile is similar to clozapine, olanzapine has a significantly lower risk of agranulocytosis. Common dose-related adverse effects include sedation, weight gain, and increased appetite, which are considered metabolic side effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_QUETIAPIN.name: Quetiapine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_QUETIAPIN.description: Quetiapine is a versatile second-generation antipsychotic with high affinity for 5-HT2, H1, 5-HT6, α1, and α2 receptors, moderate affinity for D2 receptors, and very low affinity for D1, M1, and D4 receptors. While its efficacy in treating positive symptoms of schizophrenia is relatively weak, it has a more pronounced effect on negative symptoms. Additionally, quetiapine is noted for its significant anxiolytic effects among atypical antipsychotics, with extensive research supporting its efficacy. It is also used in the treatment of refractory insomnia and can enhance the effects of antidepressants. It may have some benefit in alleviating agitation in individuals with intellectual disabilities.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_HALOPERID.name: Haloperidol
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_HALOPERID.description: Haloperidol is a high-potency typical (first-generation) antipsychotic medication from the butyrophenone class, noted for its strong D2 receptor antagonism. It is highly effective in treating the positive symptoms of schizophrenia, including hallucinations, delusions, and agitation. Additionally, it is used for managing manic symptoms and behavioral disturbances, such as those associated with Tourette syndrome in children. Due to its side effect profile, particularly the risk of EPS and cardiac issues, haloperidol use has become less common in favor of newer antipsychotics with a more favorable side effect profile.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_LITHIUM.name: Lithium Salts (lithium Carbonate)
TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_LITHIUM.description: Lithium salts have various forms and exert complex physiological and pharmacological effects on the brain. The primary lithium compound used in clinical treatment is lithium carbonate, classified as a mood stabilizer. Lithium's broad and effective long-term benefits make it a widely used treatment option for bipolar disorder.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTHERAPY.name: Psychotherapy (Social Psychological Interventions)
TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTHERAPY.description: In the fields of psychiatry and clinical psychology, psychotherapy refers to a therapeutic approach based on talk therapy guided by psychological principles. It involves a process of interpersonal interaction where a socially recognized therapist helps an individual who is struggling with psychological distress or is unable to fully function.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Escitalopram
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Escitalopram, commonly known under the brand name Lexapro, is the oxalate salt form of escitalopram. It is the S-enantiomer of citalopram and exhibits a serotonin reuptake inhibition effect that is approximately 5 to 7 times stronger than that of citalopram. As a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI), escitalopram is primarily used in clinical practice for the treatment of depression.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Fluoxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Fluoxetine, commonly known by the brand name Prozac, is available in the form of fluoxetine hydrochloride capsules. It is an antidepressant that belongs to the class of selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs). Fluoxetine is widely recognized as the first SSRI to be marketed and is primarily used for the treatment of depression. Additionally, it is used as an adjunctive therapy for the treatment of bulimia nervosa and for various other mental disorders, including obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD).

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.name: Duloxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.description: Duloxetine is a phenylamine compound and a serotonin-norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (SNRI). First synthesized in the 1980s, it is used in the treatment of depression, generalized anxiety disorder, and chronic musculoskeletal pain. It is contraindicated for use in combination with monoamine oxidase inhibitors (MAOIs), as well as with other SSRIs and SNRIs.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.name: Venlafaxine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.description: Venlafaxine, classified as a bicyclic phenylethylamine compound, differs structurally from other antidepressants and anxiolytics. It is a serotonin and norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (SNRI) used to treat major depressive disorder, generalized anxiety disorder, social anxiety disorder, and panic attacks. Venlafaxine's safety and tolerability are similar to those of SSRIs, though it commonly causes nausea, more frequently than SSRIs, and also has sexual dysfunction as a side effect. Venlafaxine is one of the most commonly reported antidepressants associated with discontinuation syndrome. Additionally, it is used in the preventive treatment of migraines.

TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZOLPIDEM.name: Zolpidem
TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZOLPIDEM.description: Zolpidem, marketed under the brand name Ambien, is an imidazopyridine class non-benzodiazepine sedative-hypnotic drug. It is a short-acting hypnotic agent with potent sedative effects, making it particularly effective for patients with difficulty falling asleep. Zolpidem significantly reduces sleep onset latency in individuals with insomnia. It works by selectively agonizing the ω-1 subtype of GABA_A receptors at the benzodiazepine (BZ) site, increasing chloride ion influx and enhancing the inhibitory effects of GABA.

TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZALEPLON.name: Zaleplon
TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZALEPLON.description: Zaleplon is a non-benzodiazepine sedative-hypnotic drug that selectively agonizes the ω-1 subtype of GABA_A receptors at the benzodiazepine (BZ) site, leading to an increase in chloride ion influx and potentiation of GABA's inhibitory effects. It has a rapid onset of action and is quickly absorbed when taken orally, effectively reducing sleep onset latency. However, due to its short duration of action, zaleplon does not significantly increase total sleep time or reduce the number of awakenings throughout the night. It is primarily used for the short-term treatment of insomnia characterized by difficulty falling asleep.

TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ESZOPICLON.name: Eszopiclone
TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ESZOPICLON.description: Eszopiclone is the right-handed isomer of zopiclone and is a non-benzodiazepine cyclopyrrolone-class sedative-hypnotic. It is the first medication approved for long-term use to improve both sleep onset and sleep maintenance difficulties. It demonstrates effective treatment for insomnia in both adults and elderly patients, with a rapid onset of action and efficacy lasting up to 6 hours. Eszopiclone is well-tolerated, with no rebound insomnia or withdrawal symptoms upon discontinuation. Possible side effects include dry mouth, dizziness, and drowsiness.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANXIOLYTIC_TANDOSPIRONE.name: Tandospirone
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANXIOLYTIC_TANDOSPIRONE.description: Tandospirone is an azapirone-class anxiolytic medication with a stronger serotonergic effect compared to buspirone. It is used to treat anxiety states associated with various neuroses, such as generalized anxiety disorder, and other conditions accompanied by anxiety. Unlike benzodiazepines or antipsychotics, tandospirone operates through a different mechanism of action, and does not produce sedative, muscle relaxant, or anticonvulsant effects. Its side effects are generally milder.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM.name: Clonazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM.description: Clonazepam is a potent benzodiazepine with anxiolytic, sedative, hypnotic, and anticonvulsant properties. Benzodiazepines are known for their rapid onset, reliability, and effectiveness; however, long-term or inappropriate use may lead to addiction, dependence, and abuse. Anecdotal evidence suggests that clonazepam may be effective for depersonalization, potentially by preventing sleep deprivation and promoting sleep. It may also address symptoms such as derealization, déjà vu, and reduced attention in depersonalization. Additionally, it is used in the treatment of refractory tic disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_ALPRAZOLAM.name: Alprazolam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_ALPRAZOLAM.description: Alprazolam is a benzodiazepine of the 3-benzodiazepine class, characterized by its medium duration and potency. It provides rapid and potent anxiolytic, sedative, and hypnotic effects. Research has also indicated that Alprazolam may possess some antidepressant properties. While benzodiazepines are known for their rapid onset, reliability, and efficacy, long-term or improper use can lead to addiction, dependence, and misuse.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_MEMANT.name: Memantine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_MEMANT.description: Memantine, marketed under the brand name Ebixa, is a non-competitive NMDA receptor antagonist. It is used for the treatment of moderate to severe Alzheimer's disease-related dementia, where it may offer efficacy in managing symptoms.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_DONEPE.name: Donepezil
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_DONEPE.description: Donepezil (brand name: Aricept) is a cholinesterase inhibitor with potential cognitive-enhancing effects. It is commonly used in clinical practice for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease and various types of dementia.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_SH.name: Shu Gan Jie Yu Capsules
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_SH.description: Shu Gan Jie Yu capsules is an important compound preparation made from Hypericum perforatum and Eleutherococcus senticosus. It is used to soothe the liver, relieve depression, strengthen the spleen, and calm the mind. It is indicated for mild to moderate unipolar depression with symptoms of liver qi stagnation and spleen deficiency.。

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_WU.name: Wu Ling Capsules
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_WU.description: Wu Ling capsules are a pure traditional Chinese medicine formulation derived from the rare medicinal fungus Pachyma (Wu Ling Shen) using modern biotechnology. The capsules contain adenosine, polysaccharides, sterols, various amino acids, vitamins, and trace elements, and are effective in treating anxiety and depression. They have sedative and calming properties and are indicated for conditions such as insomnia, depressive and anxious states, migraines, and neuroses.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_BA.name: Bai Lemin Capsules
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_BA.description: Bai Lemin Capsules are a traditional Chinese medicine that has gained attention for its potential sleep-promoting effects based on clinical observations. The formulation includes ingredients such as Lily Bulb (Bai He), Eleuthero (Ciwujia), Fleeceflower Caulis (Shouwu Teng), Albizia Flower (Hehuan Hua), Mother-of-Pearl (Zhenzhumu), Gypsum (Shigao), Ziziphus Seed (Suanzaoren), Poria (Fuling), Polygala Root (Yuanzhi), Scrophularia Root (Xuanshen), Rehmannia Root (Dihuang), Ophiopogon Root (Maidong), Schisandra (Wuweizi), Juncus Pith (Dengxincao), and Salvia Root (Danshen). The capsules are believed to nourish Yin, clear heat, calm the mind, and are indicated for insomnia of the Yin deficiency with internal heat type.
